tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary focuses on sign-bit checks with constant operands. This tests range propagation from arithmetic instructions with nsw flags, where computeConstantRange with UseInstrInfo=true determines the comparison result - a different optimization path than sign-bit canonicalization.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @nsw_add_creates_known_range(i32 %x) {
#0:
  %a = add nsw i32 %x, 100
  %c = icmp slt i32 %a, 50
  ret i1 %c
}
=>
define i1 @nsw_add_creates_known_range(i32 %x) {
#0:
  %c = icmp slt i32 %x, 4294967246
  ret i1 %c
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Known tests focus on ugt predicates with sign-bit constants. This tests ule predicate with maximum unsigned value (all ones), which should fold to constant true via icmpOrInverse when the constant range covers all possible values. Different predicate direction not shown in examples.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @ule_always_true_unsigned_max(i32 %x) {
#0:
  %c = icmp ule i32 %x, 4294967295
  ret i1 %c
}
=>
define i1 @ule_always_true_unsigned_max(i32 %x) {
#0:
  ret i1 1
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:

tests.outside_scope_reason: Known tests use srem-based patterns for range inference. This tests range computation from bitwise and operation (masking sign bit), where computeConstantRange with UseInstrInfo determines the result is always non-negative, allowing foldICmpUsingConstantRanges to fold the signed comparison to constant false.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @and_mask_nonnegative_slt_fold(i32 %x) {
#0:
  %masked = and i32 %x, 2147483647
  %c = icmp slt i32 %masked, 0
  ret i1 %c
}
=>
define i1 @and_mask_nonnegative_slt_fold(i32 %x) {
#0:
  ret i1 0
}
Transformation seems to be correct!
